title: Prompt caching: request cache breakpoints, surface cache-hit usage, ledger savings
description: Spend is input-dominated (~130:1 on MiniMax-M3 calibration) and the agent loop re-sends a stable prefix (system prompt + tools + growing message history) every iteration, so prompt caching is the single biggest cost lever. The llm.Capabilities struct already advertises PromptCaching but nothing implements it. Work: (1) Anthropic adapter — set cache_control breakpoints on system prompt, tool definitions, and the conversation prefix; parse cache_creation_input_tokens / cache_read_input_tokens. (2) OpenAI adapter — caching is automatic; parse prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens. (3) OpenAI-compatible — parse cached_tokens when the endpoint reports it (verify what MiniMax returns); ensure requests are prefix-stable (stable system text, stable tool order, append-only messages) so server-side caching can engage. (4) Extend llm.Usage with cache read/write token fields, thread through Recorder/usage ledger and progress/status reporting so cost accounting reflects real (discounted) spend. (5) Audit agent runner for anything that mutates the prefix between iterations and fix it.
acceptance_criteria: llm.Usage carries CacheReadInputTokens and CacheCreationInputTokens; conformance tests cover the new fields. Anthropic adapter sets cache_control breakpoints (system + tools + message prefix) and parses cache usage; unit test asserts breakpoint placement in the serialized request. OpenAI and openai-compatible adapters parse cached-token usage details when present. Recorder/ledger and any cost reporting include cache stats. Verified prefix stability: a test (or audit note in the bead) demonstrates the agent loop request prefix is byte-stable across iterations. Recorded-corpus replay still passes. go build/vet/test, gofmt, golangci-lint clean.
notes: PR #4 open. Branch prompt-caching (worktree commit f6786d5). Close when merged. See comments for review findings and follow-ups.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #4 (merged to main). Convention decided: llm.Usage.InputTokens is INCLUSIVE of cache reads/writes (OpenAI/Gemini native; Anthropic adapter sums input+cache_read+cache_creation); CacheRead/CacheCreationInputTokens are informational subsets so all existing budget math keeps meaning 'tokens processed'. Anthropic: <=4 ephemeral breakpoints (last tool, last system block, last blocks of final two messages — rolling anchor guarantees exact prefix hit). Agent loop verified prefix-stable (guard test TestRun_PrefixStability). Spend table migration 002 adds cache columns; threaded through funnel stats, progress, status, daemon logs. PromptCaching=false (conservative) for openai-compatible until bugbot-7f0 live probe confirms MiniMax behavior.

title: Finder reliability on real repos: forced finalization, reasoning-model token caps, failure-visible stats
description: Second calibration run (M3, 16 real files, scan scoped correctly): ZERO candidates because ALL SIX finder agents' outputs failed to parse even after RunJSON repair — 'empty model output' x3 (M3 spends its completion allowance inside <think>; funnel never sets WithMaxTokens so the provider default cap applies and visible text comes back empty) and dangling prose x2 (agents hit MaxIterations 20 mid-investigation of a 16-file chunk; final text is exploration prose, not JSON). 3.79M tokens for zero signal, single agents ran up to 30min/646k tokens. WORST: the scan printed 'No findings' indistinguishable from clean code — a trust bug. Fixes: (1) forced finalization — reserve the last iteration: when iterations/budget near exhaustion, inject 'stop investigating, output ONLY the JSON now' inside the conversation instead of parsing dangling prose; (2) funnel sets explicit per-completion MaxTokens (generous for reasoning models, ~8k) and surfaces StopReason max_tokens distinctly (one continuation retry); (3) scale iterations with chunk size or drop DefaultChunkSize (30 is too large for thorough investigation in 20 iterations); (4) HONEST STATS: per-lens parse-failure counters in Stats/Result/report output — 'lens found nothing' must be distinguishable from 'lens failed'; a scan where most lenses fail must say so loudly in the summary and report, and arguably exit nonzero.
acceptance_criteria: Finder truncation triggers an in-conversation finalization turn (test: scripted client exhausts iterations, JSON still recovered); funnel sets finder/verifier MaxTokens explicitly; Stats gains per-lens failed counter surfaced in scan summary, report metadata, and progress events; a scan with >=1 failed lens prints a prominent warning (not bare 'No findings'); recorded-corpus replay and scripted suite unchanged; calibration-style rerun on this repo produces parseable finder output from every lens.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merged, main 9834497). Forced finalization: at MaxIterations the runner injects a stop-and-emit-JSON turn and takes one tool-less final completion (fires once, iteration-cap only — never on token budget); RunJSON supplies schema-aware finalize prompts. Funnel sets MaxTokens 8192 on finder/verifier so reasoning models can't return empty visible text; max-tokens truncation gets ONE continuation stitched with overlap-trim (handles repeated-prefix restarts). DefaultChunkSize 30->8 (chosen over scaling iterations: focused context + bounded cost; rationale in code). Honest stats: FinderRuns/FinderFailures/FinderBudgetStopped (budget stop != parse failure), failures in Result.Skipped as 'LOST not absent', KindLensFailed event, scan reliability banner + nonzero exit when most finders fail, daemon slogs same warning (never exits), manifests record chunk_size. Remaining validation: live M3 re-calibration to confirm the empirical zero-candidate failure is gone (next session — needs real spend).

title: Budget enforcement overshoot: in-flight agents exceed cycle budget multiples
description: Calibration run evidence: per_cycle_tokens=1.5M, actual spend 4.12M (2.7x overshoot, input-dominated: 4.09M in / 31k out). The budget gate stops LAUNCHING new agents (post-semaphore check), but agents already in flight run to completion with their own per-runner allowances, and each tool-loop iteration re-sends the whole growing conversation (no prompt caching on openai-compatible), so 4 parallel finder agents can blow far past the ceiling between gate checks. Also observed: a hypothesized candidate ('DSN injection via unvalidated database path in store.Open') had its VERIFICATION skipped at hard-stop and was dropped entirely — budget-orphaned candidates should persist as T3 suspected, not vanish. Design directions: shared budget pool checked between agent iterations (agent.Limits hook or shared atomic pool the Runner consults pre-turn); derive per-runner budgets from remaining pool at launch; persist unverified candidates as T3 with a budget-stopped marker; consider prompt-caching capability use where the provider supports it (input dominance makes caching the biggest cost lever).
acceptance_criteria: A run with TokenBudget B never exceeds ~B plus one model-call of slack per in-flight agent (test with scripted clients reporting large usage); candidates whose verification is budget-skipped persist as tier-3 status with a clear marker and surface in Result.Skipped; calibration-style rerun confirms enforcement; eval suite unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #6 (merged, main f720878). Design: agent.BudgetPool (atomic) + Limits.BudgetCheck consulted pre-turn by every Runner — in-flight runs stop at the next turn boundary with distinct TruncBudgetPool outcome; per-runner allowances clamped to pool remainder at launch. Proven bound: spend <= B + one model-call per in-flight runner (test observes 1.05x vs the 2.7x bug). Budget-orphaned candidates persist as T3 suspected (both hard-stop and mid-vote paths), surface in Result.Skipped/Stats.Suspected and report; daemon re-verification promotes surviving T3 -> T2 (review finding, fixed+tested). Budget counts InputTokens inclusive of cache reads (conservative ceiling; discounting noted as alternative, deliberately not taken). Known slack: provider retries and RunJSON repair are pre-turn gated but only successful completions charge the pool — real-dollar overshoot can modestly exceed charged bound (documented on BudgetPool).

title: Location-based candidate dedup: merge same-defect findings across lenses
description: Real-model baseline exposed this: MiniMax M3 reported the same genuine defect from multiple lenses with different titles, and triage's fingerprint (lens+title-sensitive) kept all of them — the resource-leak case scored 1tp/4fp where most FPs were duplicates of real bugs. Cross-lens duplicates also waste verifier spend (each clone gets its own 3-refuter panel). Dedup should treat same-file + nearby-line (and ideally same-root-cause) candidates as one finding regardless of which lens or title produced them: cluster by (file, line bucket) in triage, keep the highest-severity/most-specific variant as primary, record other lenses as corroboration (corroboration could even RAISE confidence), and verify the cluster once.
design: Cluster in triage AFTER per-candidate filters, BEFORE verification: group survivors by (file, line-window) ignoring lens+title. Primary = highest severity, tie-break confidence then description specificity. Other cluster members recorded as corroborating lenses on the Candidate and persisted on the Finding (new store column + migration). Keep store.Fingerprint unchanged (lens+file+line+title of the PRIMARY) to preserve suppression-memory compat. One refuter panel per cluster. Stats: keep DroppedDuplicate (exact-fingerprint within-lens), add MergedCrossLens counter. Line window: match eval LineTolerance semantics, default ~10 lines, proximity-based not fixed buckets to avoid boundary splits. Recorded manifest for resource-leak-seeded re-pinned to post-dedup scores (fp 4->1 expected, tp preserved). Confidence-raising from corroboration deferred (recorded only, not acted on).
acceptance_criteria: Triage clusters candidates by file+line-window before verification; one verifier panel per cluster; corroborating lenses recorded on the finding; recorded-corpus replay of resource-leak-seeded shows fp reduced from 4 toward 1 (the genuinely-unseeded negative-n finding) without losing the tp; scripted suite still gates at precision 1.0; dedup stats counter distinguishes within-lens vs cross-lens merges.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #9 (squash commit 255ad25). Decision: cluster in triage after per-candidate filters, by file + 10-line proximity + Jaccard description similarity >=0.18 with transitive chaining — pure line-window was rejected because the multi-bug fixture seeds two real bugs 9 lines apart and the recorded corpus has a real panic 2 lines from the fd leak; the similarity guard (genuine dupes 0.24-0.54 vs distinct defects <=0.13) is what protects distinct nearby defects. Primary = severity > confidence > description length > stable order, keeps its own store.Fingerprint so suppression memory unaffected. One refuter panel per cluster; other members' lenses persisted as CorroboratingLenses (migration 003, comma-encoded with comma->semicolon sanitization). Stats split merged_within_lens/merged_cross_lens. Acceptance met: recorded resource-leak replay 1tp/4fp -> 1tp/1fp (remaining fp is the unseeded negative-n finding), tp preserved, scripted suite gates precision 1.0, multi-bug keeps both TPs. Verifier spend on that case: 5 panels -> 2. Confidence-raising from corroboration deferred (recorded only). Reviewer noted suppression stays per-fingerprint (lens+title), so dismissing under one lens does not suppress the location for other lenses — documented trade-off, possible future bead.

title: Eval baseline: bugbot eval command, real-model corpus, calibration run
description: Close the eval loop with real models. The harness gates precision 1.000 with SCRIPTED clients only; no real-model baseline exists, so improvements (tools, memory, debate) cannot be honestly measured. Add a bugbot eval CLI command wrapping eval.RunSuite; exercise the documented recording workflow against a real model to produce a committed transcript corpus; run a calibration scan against one real OSS repo and manually triage results for a real-world precision number. This is the enabling investment for all v2 quality work.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot eval runs the builtin suite and prints the results table with a nonzero exit on gate failure; recorded-mode corpus committed under testdata for >=3 builtin cases produced from a real model session; recording workflow doc verified by actually following it; calibration run against a real repo documented (bead comment or doc) with manually-triaged precision; CI runs the scripted suite.
notes: User will supply a MiniMax M3 API key for real-model validation. MiniMax exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, so the existing openai-compatible adapter covers it via config: providers.minimax{type: openai-compatible, base_url: <minimax /v1 endpoint>, api_key_env: MINIMAX_API_KEY}, roles -> MiniMax-M3. Validation order: (1) live smoke of Complete + tool-call round-trip (conformance suite only ever ran against mocks — first real-world probe of an OpenAI-compatible backend; watch tool-call format quirks and rate limits; adapter already defaults ParallelToolCalls=false); (2) record transcript corpus for builtin eval cases; (3) calibration scan + manual triage for a real precision number. Key must live in env only, never in config or git.
close_reason: Eval baseline established with MiniMax M3 (openai-compatible adapter, key validated live). Delivered: bugbot eval CLI sharing eval.Gate with CI; live conformance probes (-tags live) all passing against real M3 incl. tool round-trip; recorded corpus committed for 3 builtin cases with replay determinism test (TestRecordedCorpusReplay); recording workflow followed end-to-end and corrected where stale. BASELINE NUMBERS: nil-deref 1tp/0fp p=1.0; clean-code canary 0 findings (zero verifier sessions); resource-leak 1tp/4fp where manual triage showed zero confabulations (cross-lens dups of real bugs + one real unseeded defect) -> spawned bugbot-j5e (dedup) and bugbot-18t (fixture hygiene). Calibration on real repo: two runs, both documented on this bead; they found three real Bugbot bugs (scan-filter ignored — fixed inline with regression test; budget overshoot — bugbot-zrr; finder parse-failure invisibility — bugbot-210). Real-repo precision vacuous (zero findings, zero FPs) pending bugbot-210. The eval loop is closed: every future prompt/pipeline change is measurable against scripted gate + recorded real-model corpus.

title: Repro false-promotion: read-only root breaks go build cache, env failures promoted as demonstrations
description: Found during orchestrator verification: the sandbox's --read-only root leaves no writable HOME, so 'go test' dies instantly with 'failed to initialize build cache: read-only file system' (exit 1, 0.13s). interpret.go's build-failure markers don't cover environment failures, so the repro stage promoted the finding to Tier 1 off an infra failure — the exact false-reproduction class the guard exists to catch. The integration test asserted Promoted but not WHY the command failed, so it passed for the wrong reason. Manual validation: --env HOME=/tmp + tmpfs size=512m makes go test genuinely compile and fail on the seeded bug.
acceptance_criteria: (1) sandbox provides writable HOME on tmpfs and larger scratch (512m); (2) interpret treats environment-failure markers (build cache init, read-only fs, no space) and runtime exit codes 125/126/127 as NOT demonstrated; (3) integration test asserts the failure output shows the repro test actually ran (test name/FAIL marker), not merely non-zero exit; (4) full suite + integration green with a real in-container compile+fail.
close_reason: Fixed at three layers: (1) sandbox sets HOME=/tmp + 512m tmpfs so language toolchains can actually run under read-only root; (2) interpret treats exit 125/126/127 and environment-failure markers as environment_error (never demonstrated) with dedicated agent feedback telling it /tmp and workspace are the writable paths; (3) integration test asserts the seeded test name appears in failure output so it can never again pass on an infra failure. Verified: unit table covers all guard paths incl. the exact production string; integration run now ~10s of real compile+fail vs 0.13s before. Lesson recorded: promotion requires evidence the test RAN, not merely non-zero exit.

title: Build internal/agent tool-loop harness
description: Implement the reusable agent execution harness in internal/agent per bugbot-v2f.5: Tool interface, read-only code tools (read_file/list_dir/grep) rooted at a repo dir with path-traversal protection, a Runner tool-call loop with MaxIterations + TokenBudget enforcement and clean partial-result truncation, RunJSON structured output with one repair round-trip, JSONL transcripts with auto-save, and a ReplayClient for offline eval. Builds on internal/llm. Stdlib + existing internal packages only.
design: Tool errors are NOT loop failures: runTool wraps them as 'ERROR: ...' tool-result messages (IsError=true) so the model can recover; only llm.Client.Complete failures and ctx cancellation abort Run with an error. Limits resolve via zero->default, negative->unlimited, so callers can disable a cap explicitly. Budget is checked both before each turn and after tools execute, so we never issue an extra expensive completion past budget. Path safety in fsRoot: reject absolute inputs + lexical Clean/HasPrefix containment + evalExistingPrefix() which EvalSymlinks the longest existing prefix and re-checks containment, defeating symlinked intermediate dirs while tolerating not-yet-existing tail components. grep skips symlinked dirs/files (no following out of root), NUL-detects binaries, bounds results/line-length/file-size. RunJSON repair is a fresh second Run with the parse error injected (keeps the loop bounded by the same Limits) rather than mutating the first conversation. ReplayClient matching is lenient on message text but validates the trailing tool-result-ID sequence per step, erroring on divergence or exhaustion -- the offline-eval building block. Did NOT import internal/ingest: reimplemented a small glob (glob.go) to keep the dependency graph clean, matching its documented subset.
acceptance_criteria: go build/vet/test -race/gofmt all clean tree-wide. Tools reject path traversal (abs paths, .. escapes, symlink escapes) with table-driven tests. Loop truncates cleanly on iteration cap and token budget with Outcome{Truncated:true}. Tool errors fed back to model as ERROR-prefixed tool results, not loop failures. RunJSON repair path tested both ways. Transcript record->replay round-trip tested. One e2e test grep/reads a fixture tree and answers via scripted fake client, asserting tools executed and transcript captured every step.
close_reason: Built the internal/agent tool-loop harness per bugbot-v2f.5. Delivered Tool interface, traversal-safe read_file/list_dir/grep tools, Runner with MaxIterations+TokenBudget partial-result truncation, RunJSON with one repair round-trip, JSONL transcripts with auto-save, and an exported ReplayClient for offline eval. All acceptance criteria met: traversal/symlink-escape rejected, clean truncation on both caps, tool errors fed back to model, RunJSON both paths tested, record->replay round-trip verified, e2e grep/read against fixture asserts tools executed and transcript captured every step. go build/vet/test -race/gofmt clean tree-wide; 82.6% coverage. Stayed within internal/agent + stdlib + internal/llm; no go.mod changes.

title: Sandbox: pluggable interface + Docker backend
description: Core-from-day-one sandboxed execution: Sandbox interface (Exec(spec) -> result) with pluggable backends, Docker first (podman compatible). Repo snapshot mounted as workspace; no network by default; CPU/memory/time limits enforced.
acceptance_criteria: Runs a fixture repo's test suite inside a container with limits enforced; network egress verified blocked; interface mockable so funnel tests run without Docker.
close_reason: Sandbox built and verified: CLI-driven backend (os/exec to podman/docker, auto-detect) instead of Docker API client — zero deps, runtime-agnostic, works with podman 5.8.2 on this host. Security posture: workspace copy never the original, network=none default, cap-drop ALL, read-only root, no-new-privileges, pids-limit, mem/cpu caps, container reaped via rm -f on timeout. Non-zero exit is data not error; infra failures are errors. Mock exported as part of contract for funnel/repro tests. Unit tests race-clean; 6 integration tests pass against podman incl. wget blocked under network=none. Orchestrator re-ran both suites green.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text:
Sandbox execution layer implemented in internal/sandbox/ (stdlib only, no go.mod changes).

API: Sandbox interface { Exec(ctx, Spec) (Result, error) }. Spec carries RepoDir, Cmd, Env, Image, CPUs, MemoryMB, Timeout, Network, WriteFiles. Result carries ExitCode, Stdout/Stderr (1MiB-capped + Truncated flags), Duration, TimedOut. Backends: NewCLI(runtime,image,...Option) shells out to podman/docker via os/exec; NewMock(...) scriptable+call-recording for funnel/repro tests; Detect() (runtime,ok) for graceful skip. Auto-detect order: podman then docker.

Security posture (defense in depth, encoded in buildRunArgs): original repo copied to a fresh temp workspace and never mounted; workspace is the ONLY rw mount (-v ws:/workspace:rw,Z), --read-only root + tmpfs /tmp, --cap-drop ALL, --security-opt no-new-privileges, --pids-limit (default 256, fork-bomb resistance), --network=none default, --memory/--cpus limits, --rm always. WriteFiles paths sanitized (absolute/.. rejected) to prevent workspace escape. Non-zero exit is NOT a Go error (callers interpret); only infra failures error out. Timeout via context.WithTimeout; on expiry ExitCode=-1, TimedOut=true, and container reaped by generated name via rm -f.

Verified: go build/vet (both default and -tags integration) clean, gofmt clean, unit tests pass under -race. All 6 integration tests pass against podman 5.8.2: echo exit0+stdout, exit 3 -> ExitCode 3, sleep>2s timeout reaped, wget under --network=none fails, WriteFiles injection, host repo unmutated. Zero leaked containers after run.

title: Detection funnel: hypothesize, triage, verify, rank
description: The precision pipeline: Hypothesize (parallel finder agents with distinct lenses — null-safety, concurrency, error-handling, resource leaks, boundary conditions, API-contract misuse — on the cheap model role), Triage (dedup, cheap kills, suppression filter), Verify (adversarial refuter agents on the strong model role, majority-refuted candidates die), rank and assign confidence tiers T2/T3 (T1 requires repro stage).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot scan over a fixture repo with seeded bugs produces T2 findings that survive refutation; known-clean code yields zero reported findings; suppressed findings are not re-reported; per-stage counts and spend logged.
close_reason: Funnel built and verified (scripted-client e2e over fixture git repos: seeded bug survives refutation and persists as exactly one anchored T2 finding; bogus candidate killed by majority; clean-code path yields zero findings with zero verifier calls; suppression drop, dedup-across-lenses, and budget degradation all asserted). Key decisions: yield-ordered lenses drive degradation (top-2 survive); strict-majority refutation kills, tie survives; conservative parse-failure defaults in both directions; budget gate inside worker post-semaphore so degradation sees live spend; Killed tracked explicitly so budget-skips aren't miscounted; file-backed test DBs because pooled :memory: conns don't share schema. --include-suspected plumbed but inert until report stage owns T3 filtering.

title: Embedded state store (SQLite)
description: Durable state for continuous operation: findings (anchored to commit + file content hash), suppression memory, scan watermarks/file fingerprints, token spend ledger. Schema migrations from empty DB.
acceptance_criteria: Migrations apply cleanly from empty; CRUD covered by tests; findings queryable by code version; suppression lookup API used by triage; spend ledger supports per-cycle and per-day rollups.
close_reason: SQLite store built and verified (21 tests, -race). Key decisions: suppression enforcement lives INSIDE UpsertFinding's transaction — a fingerprint ever suppressed is forced to dismissed status on any future upsert, so FPs never resurface; AddSuppression is preemptive and idempotent. Findings anchored to commit_sha+file_hash enabling daemon re-verification (compare stored vs current hash). Upsert preserves id/created_at for re-found bugs. Pragmas via DSN apply to all pooled conns (WAL, busy_timeout, _txlock=immediate). RFC3339Nano UTC TEXT timestamps. ULID-like stdlib IDs, no new deps.

comments.text: Implemented internal/store/ complete. 6 source files + 5 test files (21 tests, all pass under -race; gofmt clean; vet clean). Schema: findings/suppressions/file_state/scan_runs/spend + schema_migrations, embedded versioned migrations (001_init.sql). Key decisions recorded in code doc comments: (1) suppression memory enforced inside UpsertFinding - a fingerprint that has ever been suppressed is forced to dismissed, never resurfaces as open; (2) findings anchored to commit_sha+file_hash, re-verification flow = diff current file hash vs stored; (3) timestamps stored as RFC3339Nano TEXT in UTC for lexicographic sort; (4) ULID-like time-ordered hex IDs, no external dep; (5) per-cycle budget = TotalsForScanRun, per-day = TotalsSince. NOT closing yet - leaving for human review since this is P1 IN_PROGRESS.

title: Ingest layer: repo model, fingerprinting, git polling, blast radius
description: Build the navigable model of the target repo: file tree + language detection, content fingerprinting for incremental scans, git polling for new commits/PRs (no webhook infra), and diff blast-radius computation (changed files plus direct dependents) to scope targeted investigations.
acceptance_criteria: Fingerprint diff between two commits yields correct changed-set; polling detects new commits in a test repo; blast radius computed for a sample diff includes dependents of changed files.
close_reason: Ingest built and verified (14 tests, -race, fixture git repos with isolated HOME). Key decisions: content SHA-256 fingerprints (not git blob OIDs) so watermark diffing is path/history-independent and complements commit-range ChangedFiles; ScanFilter local type decouples ingest from config evolution; Go blast radius matches imports by repo-relative dir suffix (works without module path knowledge), one hop, documented precision limits; textual fallback is whole-word basename match, recall aid not dependency analysis; Poller establishes silent baseline on empty lastSeen, tolerates offline fetch, degrades to current-tip on history rewrite. Renames via -M keep OldPath+Path; copies surfaced as adds.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Ingest layer implemented in internal/ingest/ (stdlib-only, git via os/exec). API: Open/Root, Snapshot(ctx, ScanFilter)->*Snapshot{Commit,Files[]{Path,Language,Size}}, HeadCommit, Fingerprints(ctx,*Snapshot)->map[path]sha256hex, DiffFingerprints/HashBytes helpers, ChangedFiles(ctx,from,to)->[]Change{Kind,Path,OldPath} (-z name-status, -M rename detection), ChangedPaths flattener, Poller.Poll(ctx,lastSeen)->PollResult{HeadSHA,NewCommits[]Commit} (auto remote-fetch vs local HEAD; empty lastSeen = silent baseline), BlastRadius(ctx,*Snapshot,changed)->[]string (Go import graph one-hop + textual basename fallback for non-Go). ScanFilter is local (not config.Scan) to avoid coupling; funnel maps config.Scan->ScanFilter. Decisions: fingerprints are content sha256 not git blob OIDs; package keyed by repo-relative dir suffix so module root import path is unneeded; copies modeled as adds, renames keep both paths; fetch failure tolerated (offline). Limits: blast radius is one hop, ignores build tags/cgo/reflection; textual fallback skips basenames <3 chars and over-matches common names. All tests pass incl -race, vet clean, gofmt clean (package-scoped only).

title: Agent execution harness: tool loop, tools, budgets, transcripts
description: Agent loop atop the provider layer: tool registry (read_file, grep, list_dir; symbol lookup later), tool-call iteration with max-iteration and token-budget enforcement, full transcript capture for debugging and eval fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: An agent with read/grep tools completes a code-comprehension task against a fixture repo; exceeding budget truncates cleanly with partial-result semantics; transcripts persisted and replayable.
close_reason: Harness built and verified (full tree green, -race, 82.6% pkg coverage). Decisions: tool errors are model-recoverable tool results, only Complete/ctx failures abort; truncation is data (Outcome.Truncated) not error; budget checked pre-turn AND post-tools so no completion is issued past budget; path safety = lexical containment + EvalSymlinks on longest existing prefix; RunJSON repair is a fresh bounded Run not conversation mutation; ReplayClient validates tool-result-ID sequence strictly, text leniently. Note: subagent also logged duplicate bead bugbot-57r (closed) for the same work.

title: Bugbot v1: autonomous bug-finding harness
description: Continuous, autonomous agentic harness that finds and reports bugs in a target codebase with high precision. Go, CLI-first single binary, sandbox as core feature. Pipeline: Ingest -> Hypothesize -> Triage -> Verify -> Reproduce -> Rank -> Report, with confidence tiers (T1 Reproduced / T2 Verified / T3 Suspected, T3 suppressed by default). Provider-agnostic LLM layer with role->model tiering. Full scoping rationale on bugbot-7mk.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot scan runs the full funnel on a repo and emits T1/T2 findings as markdown+SARIF; bugbot daemon runs continuously with token budgets, commit-triggered investigations, and idle backoff; dismissed findings persist as suppressions and are not re-reported; eval harness reports precision/recall on seeded-bug fixtures.
close_reason: Bugbot v1 complete: all 11 workstreams built, tested, committed. Single static binary with init/scan/daemon/report; precision-first funnel (6 lenses -> triage -> 3-refuter adversarial verify -> T2) with budget degradation; sandboxed repro promotion to T1 (podman/docker CLI backend, network=none, cap-drop ALL); SQLite state with transactional suppression memory; markdown+SARIF reporting with dismiss->suppress; daemon with polling, sweeps, day/cycle budgets, idle backoff, re-verification and auto-close; offline eval harness gating precision 1.000 on the built-in suite. Full tree green under -race (12 packages), integration suites pass against real podman. One real bug found and fixed during final verification (bugbot-64f: env failures promoted as repro demonstrations). Not yet done (future work): GitHub/webhook sinks, bugbot eval CLI command, real-model transcript corpus, SARIF schema validation in CI.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Published: https://github.com/dpoage/bugbot (public, default branch main). License decision: AGPL-3.0 — user initially proposed LGPL; recommended against it because Bugbot is an application (not a linked library) and Go's static linking makes LGPL's relink obligations awkward; user chose AGPL-3.0 so SaaS deployments must share modifications. README added with quickstart, tier table, provider config example, and dev commands.

title: Project scaffold: Go module, CLI skeleton, config, CI
description: Bootstrap the Go project: module layout, cobra CLI with stub subcommands (init, scan, daemon, report), YAML config loading with env-var overrides (provider keys never in config file), golangci-lint + go test in CI, single static binary build.
acceptance_criteria: Repo builds a single static binary; bugbot init writes a starter config; all subcommand stubs wired and print help; lint and tests pass in CI.
close_reason: Scaffold built and verified: static binary via CGO_ENABLED=0 (modernc.org/sqlite chosen for pure-Go), cobra CLI with all subcommand stubs, typed config (providers/roles/budgets/scan/sandbox/report/daemon/storage) with env overrides, deps pinned (anthropic-sdk-go v1.50.0, openai-go/v3 v3.39.0, genai v1.59.0, cobra v1.10.2, sqlite v1.52.0). internal/bootstrap/deps.go temporarily pins deps for parallel downstream work. ARCHITECTURE.md documents pipeline. All gates green.

title: LLM provider abstraction layer
description: Thin in-house provider-agnostic layer: Complete(messages, tools, opts) interface; per-provider capability profiles (context window, parallel tool calls, prompt caching, structured output); role->model tiering config mapping pipeline roles (finder/verifier/reproducer) to provider+model, mixable across providers; retry/backoff and rate-limit normalization; per-call usage accounting. Adapters: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, generic OpenAI-compatible (covers Ollama/vLLM/Groq/Mistral/OpenRouter).
acceptance_criteria: All four adapters pass a shared conformance test suite (tool-call round-trip, error normalization, usage reporting); roles resolve from config across mixed providers; degraded path works for models without parallel tool calls.
close_reason: Provider layer built and verified: thin in-house adapters (Anthropic SDK, openai-go/v3, genai, OpenAI-compatible via base URL) behind normalized Client interface with Capabilities profiles. Conformance suite 24 subtests via httptest mocks. Decisions: vendor retries disabled so WithRetry is sole retry authority; Retry-After honored where SDK exposes http.Response (Anthropic/OpenAI), genai falls back to backoff; degraded path serializes to first tool call, openai-compatible defaults ParallelToolCalls=false; tool schemas raw json end-to-end; Gemini tool results matched by name=ToolCallID since IDs not echoed; typed-nil *http.Client interface pitfall documented in code. Non-streaming Complete per spec.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Started build of internal/llm. SDK survey complete: all three SDKs (anthropic v1.50.0, openai-go/v3 v3.39.0, genai v1.59.0) support httptest redirection via base-URL + custom *http.Client options (anthropic/openai: option.WithBaseURL+WithHTTPClient+WithMaxRetries; genai: ClientConfig.HTTPOptions.BaseURL + HTTPClient). Error types: anthropic.Error & openai.Error are both *apierror.Error with StatusCode int and Response *http.Response (Retry-After readable); genai.APIError is a VALUE type with Code int and NO http.Response (Retry-After not available, backoff only). genai FunctionDeclaration.ParametersJsonSchema accepts raw JSON schema (any).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text:
internal/llm built and green. Package: 8 source files + 3 test files. Public API: Client interface (Complete + Capabilities); Request/Response/Message/ToolDef/ToolCall/Usage/StopReason/Capabilities types; NewClient(ctx, provider, providerName, model, apiKey, Options) and ResolveRole(ctx, cfg, role, Options); wrappers WithRetry/WithRecorder/WithSerializedToolCalls; typed errors ErrRateLimited/ErrAuth/ErrContextTooLong/ErrInvalidRequest/ErrServer/ErrOverloaded + *APIError (Kind/StatusCode/RetryAfter/Provider, multi-Unwrap). Decoration order recorder->retry->adapter. Conformance suite: 6 cases x 4 adapters = 24 subtests via httptest mock servers (no real keys). go build/vet/test/gofmt all clean on ./internal/llm/...

Design decisions worth recording on the consuming issue:
1. httptest redirection works for all 3 SDKs: anthropic/openai via option.WithBaseURL+WithHTTPClient+WithMaxRetries(0); genai via ClientConfig.HTTPOptions.BaseURL + HTTPClient. SDK adapter option structs take *http.Client (not the SDKs' HTTPClient interface) to avoid the typed-nil-interface panic in the anthropic SDK's no-credentials middleware.
2. Vendor SDK retries disabled (WithMaxRetries(0)) so WithRetry is the single retry authority. genai has no completion-path retry anyway.
3. Retry-After: honored for anthropic/openai (both expose *http.Response on their *apierror.Error). genai.APIError is a VALUE type with Code int and NO http.Response, so Retry-After is unavailable there -> backoff fallback (asserted in tests).
4. Degraded path (WithSerializedToolCalls): truncates multi tool-call responses to the first call so the agent loop never sees parallelism; no-op passthrough when ParallelToolCalls=true. openai-compatible defaults to ParallelToolCalls=false (unknown backend).
5. Tool schemas carried as json.RawMessage end-to-end; genai uses FunctionDeclaration.ParametersJsonSchema (raw, lossless); anthropic decodes properties/required into ToolInputSchemaParam; openai passes the schema map to FunctionDefinitionParam.Parameters.
6. Gemini tool-result mapping: function name set to ToolCallID (genai matches on name; Gemini doesn't reliably echo IDs) and result wrapped under output/error key.
7. ErrContextTooLong detected heuristically from 400 message fragments (provider-specific phrasing) + 413.

title: Scope Bugbot architecture
description: Define the architecture for Bugbot: an agentic harness that autonomously finds and reports bugs in a target codebase. Core tensions: report precision (false positives erode trust), reproduction/verification depth, and provider-agnostic LLM support with pluggable providers. This bead accumulates design decisions from the initial scoping discussion.
design:
DECIDED (2026-06-09 scoping discussion):
(1) Run model: continuous + autonomous. Bugbot runs as a long-lived process that scans the whole project with no external activity, AND ingests PRs/commits as triggers for targeted investigations. One-shot CLI scan also supported (same funnel, single pass).
(2) Language: static language — Go or Rust (Go recommended, pending confirmation; rationale: IO/LLM-bound workload, goroutine fit for agent fan-out, official Anthropic/OpenAI/Google Go SDKs, Docker SDK is native Go, single-binary distribution).
(3) Sandbox: core feature from day one, designed as pluggable backend interface (docker/podman first). Repro execution is the Tier-1 differentiator.
(4) Interface: CLI-first, single binary, users deploy as they please. Subcommand sketch: scan / daemon / report (list|show|dismiss) / init.

PIPELINE (agreed): Ingest -> Hypothesize (diverse-lens finder agents, cheap models) -> Triage (dedup, cheap kills) -> Verify (adversarial refuters, strongest model) -> Reproduce (sandboxed failing test) -> Dedup/Rank -> Report.
Confidence tiers: T1 Reproduced (executed repro artifact), T2 Verified (survived refutation w/ concrete trace), T3 Suspected (suppressed by default).
Provider layer: thin in-house adapter (complete(messages, tools, opts)), capability profiles per provider, role->model tiering (finder/verifier/reproducer), adapters: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, generic OpenAI-compatible.
Reports: markdown + SARIF; suppression memory persists FP dismissals and feeds triage.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc or design fields capture: (1) pipeline stages and verification strategy, (2) LLM provider abstraction approach, (3) language/stack choice, (4) report format and confidence model, (5) sandboxing/repro strategy. Decisions recorded with rationale.
notes: Language CONFIRMED: Go. Rationale: IO-bound LLM orchestration, goroutine fan-out fit, official provider SDKs in Go, native Docker SDK for sandbox, single static binary for CLI-first deployment.
close_reason: Scoping complete; all five acceptance items decided and recorded in design field. Decisions: (1) funnel pipeline with adversarial verification and T1/T2/T3 confidence tiers, T3 suppressed by default; (2) thin in-house provider abstraction with capability profiles and role->model tiering, adapters for Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/OpenAI-compatible; (3) Go confirmed over Rust (IO-bound orchestration, official SDKs, native Docker SDK, single binary); (4) markdown+SARIF reports with persistent suppression memory; (5) sandbox core from day one as pluggable interface, Docker backend first. Run model: continuous daemon + one-shot CLI. Implementation broken down under epic bugbot-v2f.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Continuous mode implications identified: needs embedded state store (SQLite) for findings/suppressions/watermarks; incremental scanning via file fingerprinting; re-verify findings when implicated code changes; cost governance (token budgets per cycle/day, backoff when repo idle); event sources start with git polling (no webhook infra required, fits CLI-first), webhook listener optional later; report sinks pluggable (fs/SARIF, GitHub issues, webhook, stdout).

title: False-positive memory: dismissals feed refuters as few-shot context
description: Make precision compound with use instead of resetting every scan. New 'bugbot dismiss <id> --reason' records a human dismissal (pattern, file, lens, fingerprint, reason) in the store; at verify time, refuters for a candidate get the K most relevant past dismissals (same lens, similar pattern/file) injected as few-shot 'previously dismissed because...' context. Re-surfacing a dismissed fingerprint is suppressed outright (suppression already exists — extend it with reasons). Relevance can start dumb (same lens + same file or package) and get smarter later. Synergy: the hash-anchored KV store planned in bugbot-dtq is the natural substrate; build on it.
acceptance_criteria: dismiss command persists reason + metadata; suppressed fingerprints never re-surface (existing behavior preserved, now with visible reason in report); refuter prompt for a candidate includes relevant dismissals when they exist (unit test asserts injection + relevance filter); recorded-corpus replay unaffected; tree green + lint clean.

title: Patch-prover: minimal candidate fix as a tier-0 confidence witness
description: Repro already demonstrates a bug with a failing sandboxed test; go one further: an agent writes a MINIMAL candidate fix patch, and the sandbox proves the failing test passes with the patch applied (and the rest of the suite still passes). A finding shipped with failing-test + working-patch is nearly review-free — promote to a new tier 0 ('demonstrated + fix witness'). The patch is a witness, not a recommendation: emit it in the report as a diff block with clear 'starting point, not reviewed' framing. Also a brutal implicit refuter: if no plausible minimal fix exists after N attempts, flag the finding for human review (might be misdiagnosed). Scope: Go targets first, reuse the repro sandbox machinery.
acceptance_criteria: Repro stage optionally attempts a fix patch after a demonstrated failure; sandbox verifies failing-test-passes + suite-still-green with patch applied; promoted findings carry tier 0 and the diff in report output; fix-not-found after N attempts marks the finding needs-human with the attempt summary; integration test gated like existing sandbox tests; tree green + lint clean.

title: PR review mode: blast-radius scan posts inline review comments
description: Catch bugs pre-merge where they are cheapest. 'bugbot review --pr N': resolve the PR's base/head via gh, run the existing --since blast-radius targeted scan over the head checkout, then post verified findings as inline PR review comments (gh api) anchored to the diff positions, with the reasoning trace collapsed in a <details> block. The funnel's adversarial verification is the differentiator vs typical AI reviewers — only T2+ goes to the PR; suspected T3 goes to a single summary comment (or is withheld, config). Re-running on a new push must update/resolve stale comments via fingerprint, not duplicate. CI-friendly: nonzero exit on new T2 findings (configurable) so it can gate merges.
design: New cobra command internal/cli/review.go following scan.go pattern. PR resolution via 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/N' (gh auto-fills owner/repo placeholders — no resolution code). Require repo HEAD == PR head SHA (CI-friendly; error with guidance otherwise). Changed files via repo.ChangedFiles(baseSHA, headSHA) -> ChangedPaths -> funnel.Targeted (blast radius included). Inline comments: parse 'git diff base...head' hunks to compute RIGHT-side commentable lines; findings on commentable lines -> inline review comments (POST pulls/N/comments with commit_id/path/line/side), others + T3 suspected -> single summary issue comment. Lifecycle: hidden HTML marker '<!-- bugbot:fp=... -->' in each comment body; re-run lists existing comments, builds fp->id map, PATCHes changed bodies, creates new, marks stale ones resolved via body edit (no GraphQL thread resolution). Summary comment keyed by '<!-- bugbot:summary -->' marker, updated in place, includes spend/reliability stats from funnel.Stats. gh execution behind an injectable runner interface so tests fake responses. Config: new review section (fail_on: verified|none, suspected: summary|withhold), flags override. Exit: nonzero on new T2 findings per fail_on; also keep MostFindersFailed unreliable-scan error.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot review --pr N posts inline comments on a real or fixture PR mapped to correct diff lines; re-run after a push updates rather than duplicates (fingerprint match); summary comment includes spend/reliability stats; configurable exit gate; unit tests with faked gh responses; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #10 (squash commit 8092e0d). Decisions: all GitHub I/O via 'gh api' behind an injectable ghRunner func-type seam (auth delegated to gh, tests fake with canned JSON; {owner}/{repo} placeholders mean zero repo-resolution code). Local HEAD must equal PR head SHA — no auto-checkout, error gives exact fetch/checkout commands (CI checkout is the target env). Commentable lines computed locally from 'git diff -M base...head' hunk parsing (three-dot, matching GitHub's anchor base); T2 on commentable lines -> inline review comments, blast-radius T2 outside diff + T3 -> single summary issue comment. Lifecycle keyed by hidden first-line markers: bugbot:fp=<fingerprint> per finding, bugbot:summary for the summary, bugbot:resolved tags tombstones. planSync is a pure reconciler (create/update/skip/resolve) -> applyPlan is the only writer (no-op under --dry-run). Stale comments PATCHed to a SHA-free resolved notice, never deleted; detected by marker so no re-PATCH churn on later pushes. CI gate (review.fail_on verified|none, flag + BUGBOT_REVIEW_* env): nonzero on NEW T2 fingerprints not on the PR; review round caught and fixed a tombstone gate bypass — resolved fingerprints count as ABSENT so fix-then-revert trips the gate again and the comment un-resolves to the full body. Known deferred (reviewer minors): same-fp collision across review+issue comment kinds orphans the review comment (migration edge); no snapshot progress sink (bugbot status can't watch a review run); review runs persist findings into the local store (ephemeral in CI — acceptable). Acceptance met: faked-gh tests cover create/idempotent-rerun/update/stale-resolve/reappear-gate/summary-in-place/T3 routing/exit gate/dry-run/pagination; CI + all local gates green.

title: GitHub issue publishing: findings -> gh issues with lifecycle sync
description: Close the loop from findings to action. New 'bugbot publish' command (and daemon post-cycle hook) that files T2+ findings as GitHub issues by shelling out to gh: title from finding title, body with reasoning trace, repro test (when present), file:line permalink, lens/tier labels, and the finding fingerprint embedded in an HTML comment for dedup. Lifecycle sync both ways: re-publishing an already-filed fingerprint updates instead of duplicating; when daemon re-verification auto-closes a finding (code changed, bug gone), close the GH issue with a 'fixed in <sha>' comment. Respect a config allowlist (publish.tiers, publish.labels, dry-run flag). Design note: also consider SARIF as a sibling exporter (separate bead bugbot output for code-scanning UI).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot publish files issues for open T2+ findings via gh with fingerprint-comment dedup (re-run files zero duplicates); finding auto-close closes the linked issue with the fixing sha; dry-run prints without filing; works without gh installed (clear degradation error); unit tests with a fake gh shim binary; tree green + lint clean.

title: Anthropic auth via Claude coding-plan (OAuth) in addition to API keys
description: The anthropic provider currently authenticates only via api_key_env (pay-per-token API key). The user's primary Anthropic access is a Claude coding-plan subscription (the auth Claude Code uses), so the Anthropic half of the cache probe (originally bugbot-7f0: real cache hit-rates from the agent loop, 1h-TTL decision) is blocked — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on the host. Add subscription auth support: config gains an auth mode on the anthropic provider type (api_key | oauth-token), where oauth mode sends the coding-plan OAuth bearer token (e.g. sourced from an env var the user populates via 'claude setup-token', or by reading Claude Code's stored credentials — evaluate which is robust and least surprising) with the required anthropic-beta OAuth header instead of x-api-key. Everything downstream (capabilities, cache breakpoints, usage parsing) stays identical. NOTE for implementer: verify current header/beta-flag requirements for OAuth bearer tokens against the live Claude Code behavior rather than trusting training data; also check Anthropic ToS posture on third-party subscription use and surface it in the bead if it's a concern — this is a personal tool using the user's own plan.
acceptance_criteria: Config accepts an anthropic provider in oauth mode without api_key_env; adapter sends correct Authorization + beta headers (unit test on serialized request, no live call needed for the test); api_key mode unchanged (existing tests pass); clear error message when the token env is missing/expired; docs/template.go example added; live smoke: one completion against the coding plan succeeds and reports cache fields, then run the deferred Anthropic cache-hit-rate observation and record the 1h-TTL decision on this bead.

title: Live probe: verify MiniMax cached_tokens and Anthropic cache hit-rates; consider 1h TTL
description: bugbot-2ix (PR #4) parses prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens on openai-compatible endpoints but it is unverified whether MiniMax actually returns it (Capabilities.PromptCaching left false/conservative for compatible endpoints). Also unverified live: Anthropic breakpoint placement actually yields expected cache hit-rates in a real agent loop. One cheap live scan confirms both — check spend ledger cache_read_tokens > 0 after a 2+ iteration run. If MiniMax caches, consider a config override to set PromptCaching=true for known-caching compatible endpoints. Separately evaluate extended cache TTL (1h vs default 5m) for long sweeps where cycles outlive the 5m window. Live invocation recipes are in bd memories (live-eval-invocations, minimax-key-and-endpoint).
acceptance_criteria: Documented (bead comment) whether MiniMax returns cached_tokens, with raw usage JSON evidence; Anthropic hit-rate observed and recorded from a live loop (cache_read_tokens vs input_tokens per iteration); decision recorded on PromptCaching config override and on 1h TTL (implement or explicitly decline with rationale).
close_reason: MiniMax half complete with evidence; Anthropic half moved to bugbot-dbg (no Anthropic key on host — coding-plan OAuth support needed first). FINDINGS: (1) MiniMax DOES automatic prefix caching and reports prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens — direct probe, identical 7684-token request twice: cached 114 then 7666 (~99.8%); raw JSON in bead comments. (2) End-to-end on live scans: 78% and 75% cache-read rates flowing through ledger + scan summary ('Cache: read=N' line) — caching is working and accounted. (3) Budget pool live-validated: 400k budget, 453k spend (1.13x vs the old 2.7x overshoot); in-flight finders stopped at turn boundaries with honest 'partial coverage' skip notes. (4) Finder fixes validated: single-lens run completed with parseable output and zero failures (baseline: 6/6 unparseable); trustworthy 'No findings'. DECISIONS: Capabilities.PromptCaching stays false for openai-compatible (nothing gates on it; cached_tokens parsing works regardless — revisit only if a caller starts gating). 1h-TTL: deferred to bugbot-dbg (Anthropic-specific; MiniMax caching is automatic, no TTL knob). Residual: full 6-lens coverage of even a 6-file package needs ~1.5-2M cycle budget with M3; budget sizing guidance worth a note in config docs.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Scope adjustment (2026-06-09): host has only MINIMAX_API_KEY — the Anthropic half (live hit-rates, 1h TTL decision) moves to new bead bugbot-dbg (coding-plan OAuth auth support), which carries the deferred observation in its acceptance. This bead closes on the MiniMax evidence + calibration scan. MiniMax cached_tokens: CONFIRMED via direct probe — identical 7,684-token-prompt request twice: usage payload 1: {"prompt_tokens":7684,"prompt_tokens_details":{"cached_tokens":114}}; payload 2: {"prompt_tokens":7684,"prompt_tokens_details":{"cached_tokens":7666}} (~99.8% hit, automatic server-side). Adapter already parses this field. Live calibration scan over internal/lsp in progress.

title: Code-nav fallback tier: tree-sitter backend for repos without working builds
description: LSP servers vary in build requirements: pyright/tsserver work from source, but gopls needs resolvable modules, clangd needs compile_commands.json, JVM servers need full builds. On target repos where the build is broken or deps unfetchable, the LSP backend (bugbot-tmb) degrades to plain grep. Add a middle tier backed by tree-sitter (user direction 2026-06-09: tree-sitter is the intended approach; the earlier 'telescope' mention meant this): parse source directly with per-language grammars and the standard tags.scm definition/reference queries (the same mechanism behind GitHub basic code nav). Gives real parsing with no build and distinguishes call sites from comment/string mentions — the main noise source in grep fallback. Honest limit: syntactic only, no cross-file semantic resolution; find_definition returns ranked candidates for ambiguous names and the result text says so. IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINT: bugbot builds CGO_ENABLED=0 (make build) and Go tree-sitter bindings require cgo — use WASM grammars via wazero (keeps static build, in-process) or shell out to ast-grep/tree-sitter CLI as a single external binary; evaluate both, prefer the wazero route if grammar coverage suffices. ctags remains a simpler plan-B if tree-sitter integration proves heavy. Backend selection: LSP healthy -> tree-sitter -> plain grep, behind the same tool names so the model is agnostic; result text carries a confidence caveat naming the tier that answered.
acceptance_criteria: find_definition and find_references answer via tree-sitter queries when the LSP server for the language is missing or unhealthy, with a tier caveat and ranked candidates for ambiguous names; reference results exclude comment/string mentions (unit test proves it vs grep); backend selection covered by unit tests (LSP up, LSP down -> tree-sitter, grammar missing -> plain grep); CGO_ENABLED=0 build still works (make build green); integration test on a fixture repo with deliberately broken go.mod proving definitions still resolve; tree green + lint clean.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #7 (merged). Route decided: github.com/odvcencio/gotreesitter — pure-Go tree-sitter runtime (no cgo, no WASM layer); WASM/wazero and external-binary routes evaluated and rejected (heavier / reintroduces missing-binary problem). Tier: LSP healthy -> tree-sitter -> grep behind same tool names; falls back ONLY on never-answerable LSP errors, never on timeout; find_implementations stays LSP-only. References match AST call nodes so comment/string mentions excluded (tested). Review criticals fixed: per-tagger mutex (race was reproduced, test is load-bearing), recover seam for the dep's known GLR panic caps, per-file tag cache (mtime+size invalidation), distinct tsx grammar. Binary cost contained via upstream grammar_subset build tags in make build: 86.2MB -> 64.8MB. Plan-B ctags never needed.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation done (Opus agent, commit b2cbf65): route = github.com/odvcencio/gotreesitter (pure-Go tree-sitter, no cgo/WASM), tags-query backend, tiered navigator falling back only on never-answerable LSP errors. Review found CRITICAL confirmed data race (shared Tagger/Parser mutable state, reproduced under -race with 50 concurrent calls) + CRITICAL missing recover() on parse path (dep has known GLR panic caps); HIGH whole-repo reparse per query (no per-file tag cache) + HIGH .tsx mapped to plain typescript grammar (JSX silently fails); MEDIUM ~24MB embedded grammars for 4 languages (upstream has subset build tags to investigate). Clean: dependency hygiene (pure Go, benign transitives), symlink/sandbox safety (WalkDir doesn't follow; render re-checks containment), fallback triggers vs real LSP error strings, deterministic ranking, caps/dedup consistent with LSP tier. Fix agent dispatched for all findings.

title: Eval fixture hygiene: seed all real defects, stop documenting bugs in comments
description: Baseline triage showed two fixture-quality issues: (1) resource-leak fixture contains a REAL unseeded defect (make([]byte, n) with caller-controlled n panics on negative input) so honest findings score as false positives — fixtures must either seed every genuine defect they contain or eliminate the unseeded ones; (2) the fixture's doc comment literally describes its own seeded bug ('On the short-read error path it returns without closing f: a leaked file descriptor') — the model can read the answer off the comment, inflating recall. Sweep all builtin fixtures for both problems.
acceptance_criteria: Every builtin fixture audited: no genuine defect is unseeded (either added to Seeded with tolerance or code fixed); no comment/docstring reveals a seeded bug; scripted suite updated accordingly and still gates 1.0; re-record the corpus afterward and note score deltas on bugbot-9w5 (recall measured without comment-hints is the honest number).

title: Activity visibility: live progress pane and bugbot status
description: Bugbot gives no sign of life while working: scan prints only a final summary after potentially many minutes of agent activity, and the daemon logs sparsely. Operators need to see that the tool is running and what it is doing. Design: a progress-event seam (callback/channel) emitted from the funnel and daemon — stage transitions, agent run start/finish (role, lens, file chunk, candidate), live token spend, cycle schedule — consumed by pluggable renderers: (1) default TTY pane: in-place ANSI multi-line status (current stage, active agents, candidates surviving per stage, spend ticker) degrading to plain log lines when not a TTY; (2) the existing slog stream; (3) a status snapshot persisted (status file under .bugbot or store table) so a separate terminal can run 'bugbot status' against a running daemon and see current cycle, last findings, spend today, next sweep ETA. Avoid heavyweight TUI deps if feasible (plain ANSI first; bubbletea only if clearly warranted).
acceptance_criteria: bugbot scan on a TTY shows live per-stage progress (stage, active agent count, lens/candidate labels, running token spend) updating in place, and plain sequential lines when piped; daemon emits the same events through slog; bugbot status run from another terminal reports a running daemon's current activity, today's spend, open findings count, and next poll/sweep times (and says so clearly when no daemon is running); event seam is a typed interface in funnel/daemon usable by future sinks; no flicker/garbage on narrow terminals; tests cover event emission ordering and the non-TTY fallback rendering.
close_reason: Built and verified (13 packages green under -race, lint 0 issues). Decisions: emission contract is never-block/never-fail/never-panic with a single Emit choke point (nil-safe, time-stamped); pane is plain ANSI with COLUMNS fallback 80 and ModeCharDevice TTY detection — zero new deps, no bubbletea; spend ticks reuse the funnel's existing spendRecorder via an onRecord hook; snapshot writes are temp+rename atomic, ~1s rate-limited, forced on terminal events; status staleness = old last_updated OR dead pid (signal 0); repro per-attempt emission deliberately skipped to contain the diff — promotions visible at cycle level (noted for follow-up if wanted); runRefuters now returns per-candidate verifier tokens for agent_finished events.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Starting implementation. Read funnel/{funnel,run,hypothesize,verify,triage,lens,helpers}, daemon/{daemon,run,cycle,postcycle,clock}, cli/{scan,daemon,root}, store/{scanruns,spend,findings,store}, repro/promote, config, llm/recorder. Plan: new internal/progress package (events.go, sink.go, pane.go, log.go, snapshot.go, term.go), funnel Options gains Progress sink emitting at run/stage/agent/spend/budget boundaries, daemon Deps gains Progress sink for cycle/schedule/reverify/promote, CLI wires PaneRenderer (TTY) / LogRenderer (non-TTY) + Snapshot for scan, LogRenderer-over-slog + Snapshot for daemon, new 'bugbot status' command reading status.json.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete. New package internal/progress (events.go, sink.go, term.go, format.go, log.go, pane.go, snapshot.go) with full tests. Funnel Options gained Progress sink; emits scan_started/finished, stage_started/finished w/counts, agent_started/finished (finder=lens, verifier=candidate title), spend_tick (via recorder onRecord hook), budget_degraded/stopped. runRefuters signature changed to also return summed tokens (VerifyFinding updated). Daemon Deps gained Progress, threaded into per-cycle funnel via newFunnel; emits cycle_scheduled/started/finished, reverify, promote. CLI scan: TTY->PaneRenderer else LogRenderer, both + SnapshotSink, pane stopped before final summary. CLI daemon: SlogRenderer + SnapshotSink with daySpend getter. New 'bugbot status' command reads status.json (missing/stale-by-time/stale-by-dead-pid/fresh, exit 0 always, open findings count via store). All gates pass: build, vet (+tags), test -race, gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues. Promotion still visible via daemon's cycle-level KindPromote count event. Width detection is syscall-free ($COLUMNS fallback 80) per spec; TTY detection via os.ModeCharDevice.

title: Leads blackboard: cross-lens agent messaging
description: Finders trip over out-of-lens suspicions that currently evaporate (nil-safety finder notices dodgy locking). Add a leads table (poster role/lens, target lens, file/line, note, scan_run, status posted|consumed) and a post_lead tool for finders. The funnel injects pending leads into the matching lens's next task context (next chunk or next cycle in daemon mode) and marks them consumed. Deliberately asynchronous blackboard, NOT direct agent chat: persistent, deterministic, testable — and verify-stage refuters stay isolated by design since their independence is what kills false positives.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds leads table; post_lead tool for finder runners only (verifiers excluded by design, documented); funnel injects pending leads into matching lens prompts and marks consumed; dedup of near-identical leads; cross-cycle test: lens A posts a concurrency lead, next concurrency run receives it (daemon carries it across cycles); leads visible in scan stats; eval suite unchanged or better.

title: Debate verification: advocate/refuter/judge experiment
description: Experiment: replace independent-refuter majority voting with a structured debate — an advocate argues the candidate is real, a refuter argues it is not, a judge agent rules. Literature suggests debate improves judgment quality but it costs more tokens and trades away refuter independence, so it must EARN its place: implement as a pluggable verify strategy behind config and judge it with the eval harness (scripted + recorded corpora) against the majority-refutation baseline on precision, recall, and spend. Adopt, keep-as-option, or reject based on numbers; record the decision either way.
acceptance_criteria: Verify strategy is pluggable (majority|debate) via config with majority remaining default; debate implementation with bounded turns; eval comparison runnable as one command producing a side-by-side table (precision/recall/tokens per strategy); decision recorded on this bead with the numbers; if rejected, code stays behind config as an experiment, not deleted silently.

title: Memory: hash-anchored KV store + agent memory tools
description: Agents re-derive the same codebase facts every cycle — pure token waste in daemon mode. Add a memory table (key, value, provenance: role+scan_run, file anchors with content hashes, optional TTL/confidence) plus memory_get/memory_put/memory_search agent tools. Entries auto-invalidate when any anchored file's hash changes (reuses the finding re-verification machinery). Prompts must treat recalled entries as hints to verify, never ground truth — memory poisoning (one wrong fact propagating across cycles) is the failure mode; provenance + hash invalidation + hint framing are the mitigations.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds memory table; three tools with schemas wired into finder/verifier runners; invalidation test: change an anchored file, entry no longer returned; TTL expiry test; provenance recorded and shown; prompt updates frame memory as unverified hints; poisoning posture documented in ARCHITECTURE.md; eval suite unchanged or better.

title: Refutation memory: machine-learned triage suppressions
description: Suppression memory only learns from human dismissals today. When refuters kill a candidate, persist the refutation (reason, anchored to the implicated file's content hash). Triage pre-kills matching future candidates for free while the anchor holds — and the entry expires the moment the file changes, so a stale refutation can never hide a bug introduced later. Extends the suppression concept from human-taught to machine-learned with automatic expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Refuter majority-kills write refutation entries keyed by candidate fingerprint pattern + file hash; triage consults them before verification and stats count the pre-kills separately; expiry test proving a changed file re-enables full verification; eval case: same bogus candidate found twice costs verifier tokens only once; a seeded real bug introduced after a refutation of similar shape is NOT suppressed (the critical safety test).

title: Git history tools + churn heat prioritization
description: Two wins from the existing git-CLI plumbing: (1) git_blame and git_log tools for agents — bug context, regression candidates, recent-change awareness; (2) a heat model (churn x recency, later x coverage-gap) that ranks files so sweep chunking spends finder budget where bugs statistically cluster instead of uniformly.
acceptance_criteria: git_blame/git_log tools with bounded output and fixture-repo tests; heat scoring in ingest or funnel orders sweep chunks (highest heat first) with unit tests; degradation when history is shallow; ordering observable in scan stats/logs; eval suite unchanged.

title: Verifier sandbox-exec tool: evidence-based refutation
description: Only the repro stage executes code today; refuters argue rhetorically. Expose a gated sandbox execution tool to the verify stage so a refuter can empirically demonstrate a refutation (run the guard path, run the existing test suite) instead of asserting it. Converts verification from rhetoric to evidence — the core precision philosophy. Sandbox runs are expensive: gate by config flag and candidate severity, bound per-candidate executions, and record spend.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox exec tool exposed only to verifier-role runners behind a config flag with a severity gate; per-candidate execution count bounded; output capped; spend/duration recorded in scan stats; eval case demonstrating an empirical refutation flow; network stays none; full tree green.

title: Code-nav tools: LSP-backed find_definition/find_references/find_implementations (multi-language)
description: Code navigation tools for agents, LSP-first (general, not Go-only — user direction 2026-06-09, supersedes the go/packages v1 bugbot-24n). Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add find_definition / find_references (callers) / find_implementations tools to the agent harness backed by a hand-rolled LSP client (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, Content-Length framing, ~5 methods — no official Go client lib; x/tools client is internal/, community libs stale). The real work is server lifecycle: per-language spawn (gopls, typescript-language-server, pyright, clangd), initialize handshake + capability negotiation, crash restart, clean shutdown, discovery by file extension, graceful degradation when servers are missing. Simplifiers: agents are read-only (no didChange sync) and tool errors already round-trip to the model. Wire into funnel readOnlyTools(); update refuter prompt to prefer find_references when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: LSP client speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with correct Content-Length framing and UTF-16 position conversion (non-ASCII test case required). Tools registered with JSON schemas and wired into finder/verifier tool sets. Server lifecycle managed: spawn, initialize handshake, bounded warm-up wait (never hang; degrade to a clear 'unavailable' tool error), crash restart, clean shutdown. Language server discovery by extension with graceful degradation message when binary missing. Unit tests with a fake/scripted server for protocol + lifecycle; integration tests gated on real server availability (gopls; skip cleanly when absent) like sandbox tests. Refuter prompt updated. go build/vet/test and gofmt clean; golangci-lint clean.
notes: PR #5 open, branch lsp-code-nav (commit fdd6167). Close when merged. Gotcha: gopls zero-references on interface methods not called via interface. Follow-up beads: repro tool set lacks code-nav; daemon-lifetime LSP manager for warm indexes; bugbot-0ze tree-sitter fallback.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #5 (merged to main 9edf9a6). LSP-first per user direction, superseding go/packages v1. Hand-rolled 5-method JSON-RPC client (internal/lsp), lifecycle: lazy per-language spawn, capability gating, crash-restart-once-then-disable, bounded 60s queries, polite shutdown with kill escalation. Tools take {file,line,symbol} — tool resolves column itself. UTF-16 verified vs live gopls (emoji/CJK) and clangd. Post-review hardening: symlink containment on result paths, 16KB header bound, -race concurrency tests. Known gotcha: gopls returns zero references for interface methods never called through the interface. Follow-ups: bugbot-0ze (tree-sitter fallback), bugbot-yc8 (repro tools), bugbot-cy8 (daemon-lifetime manager), bugbot-ya3 (conn robustness lows).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete on PR #5 (https://github.com/dpoage/bugbot/pull/5). All gates green locally (build/vet/test/gofmt, CI-exact golangci-lint 0 issues, -race clean on lsp+agent, integration vs real gopls AND clangd incl. UTF-16 emoji/CJK case). Merges cleanly with PR #4 in either order (merge-tree verified, 0 conflict markers). Design: new internal/lsp package (jsonrpc framing / conn with server-request answering / server lifecycle / manager with crash-restart-then-disable); tools take {file,line,symbol} and resolve the column themselves so the model never guesses columns. Findings fixed in fb1189c: H1 symlink content-leak on result paths (relPath now mirrors fsRoot.resolve symlink containment), M3 unbounded header read loop (16KB budget), L6 misleading indexing-timeout message on parent deadlines, M2+L7 missing concurrency/-race coverage (4 new tests: concurrent conn calls w/ shuffled responses, transport death racing senders, concurrent lazy spawn, queries across crash-restart; FAKE_LSP_STALL_ON knob replaces the nanosecond-timeout hack). L4+L5 (read-loop reply wedge, stderr-tail vs EOF race) deferred to bugbot-ya3.
