All gates re-run green after fixes.

title: Code-nav tools v1: find_definition/find_callers via go/packages (Go)
description: Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add call-graph navigation tools to the agent harness: find_definition, find_callers (and find_implementations for interfaces), with language-agnostic tool names/schemas and a Go backend via golang.org/x/tools/go/packages (new dep, acceptable). Wire into finder and verifier runners when the target repo is Go. Update the refuter prompt to prefer find_callers when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: Tools registered for Go repos with JSON schemas; table-driven tests on a fixture package covering functions, methods, and interface implementations; graceful degradation message for non-Go targets; refuter prompt updated; eval suite precision unchanged or better; full tree green.

title: Implement internal/repro: Tier-1 reproduce stage
description: Build the Reproduce stage per bugbot-v2f.8. Reproducer agent (read-only tools) generates a minimal failing test for a T2 finding, executes it in the sandbox (network none), interprets exit codes, guards against compile-error false reproductions, writes artifacts (test files + run.sh + README.md) on success, promotes finding to T1 with repro_path. PromoteAll runs bounded-parallel over T2 findings. Wire a --repro flag into internal/cli/scan.go.
acceptance_criteria: internal/repro package with Reproducer.New, Attempt, PromoteAll, Summary; go vet ./internal/repro/... ./internal/cli/...; go test -race ./internal/repro/... green; success/zero-exit-revision/compile-guard/exhaustion unit tests with fake llm.Client + sandbox.NewMock + real store in t.TempDir; optional integration test behind build tag. gofmt clean.
close_reason: Implemented internal/repro: Reproducer{New,Attempt,PromoteAll,Summary,Plan,Attempt,FindingOutcome,Options}. Reproducer agent uses read-only tools (read_file/list_dir/grep) + RunJSON with a plan schema {files,cmd,expect}; system prompt demands a minimal assertion-bearing failing test that fails on buggy code and passes when fixed. Execution via sandbox Spec network=none. interpret() promotion rules: exit0=>not demonstrated, timeout=>not, non-zero with build-failure markers=>not (false-repro guard), else demonstrated. Non-demonstrating attempts feed category-specific feedback back for revision up to MaxAttempts. On success writes ArtifactDir/<id>/ bundle (repro files + run.sh + README.md) and PromoteAll updates store to T1 + repro_path via a SQL-free wrapper over GetFindingByFingerprint+UpsertFinding (no new store method needed). Failure leaves finding at T2 and removes partial artifacts. PromoteAll bounded by MaxParallel (default 2). Wired --repro into cli/scan.go with sandbox.Detect graceful skip. Tests: success, zero-exit->revision->success, compile-error guard, exhaustion (all real store in t.TempDir, sandbox.NewMock, scripted fake client) + integration test behind build tag 'integration' with real podman/golang image and hand-written plan. go vet + go test -race green, gofmt clean.

title: Reporting layer: report pkg (md/SARIF emitters, sinks) + report CLI
description: Build internal/report (Markdown + SARIF 2.1.0 emitters over []store.Finding+metadata, Sink interface with FSSink/StdoutSink, SinksFromConfig registry) and rewrite internal/cli/report.go (list/show/dismiss/emit). Package must stay independent of funnel to avoid import cycles for daemon reuse.
acceptance_criteria: go vet + go test -race ./internal/report/... green; gofmt clean on new files; SARIF unmarshals with required non-empty fields and severity->level mapping; markdown golden test deterministic; dismiss writes suppression (IsSuppressed true) and list --status dismissed shows it; ID-prefix resolution handles exact/prefix/ambiguous.
close_reason: Built internal/report (report.go core/sort, markdown.go, sarif.go hand-rolled 2.1.0 structs, sink.go FSSink+StdoutSink+SinksFromConfig, query.go CollectOpen+ResolveID) and rewrote internal/cli/report.go with list/show/dismiss/emit. Dismiss uses store.AddSuppression (atomic suppress+flip) per spec. SinksFromConfig accepts fs/stdout and treats legacy markdown/sarif as FSSink aliases (config default ships 'markdown') collapsing to one FSSink. Package is funnel-independent (operates on store.Finding + small findingLister iface) so daemon/scan can reuse via report.CollectOpen. Tests: markdown golden, SARIF shape+severity mapping+repo-relative URIs, FSSink latest copies, full dismiss flow vs real store, ID-prefix resolution (exact/prefix/ambiguous), CLI end-to-end. go vet + go test -race + gofmt all green on owned packages; go build ./... succeeds.

title: Daemon mode: scheduler, budgets, backoff, re-verification
description: bugbot daemon: scheduler interleaving the slow whole-repo sweep with commit/PR-triggered targeted investigations (blast-radius scoped); token budgets per cycle and per day with graceful degradation (fewer lenses, defer deep verification); idle backoff when repo unchanged; re-verify open findings whose implicated code changed and auto-close fixed ones.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon detects a new commit in a fixture repo and runs a targeted investigation; budget exhaustion halts LLM spend with a clear log line; a fixed seeded bug is auto-closed on the next cycle; idle repo produces near-zero spend.
close_reason: Daemon built and verified (6 tests, -race, injectable clock so no wall-time sleeps: commit->targeted-scan flow, idle backoff growth, day-budget skip with zero funnel calls, auto-close of fixed findings, graceful shutdown). Decisions: re-verify seam = new additive funnel.VerifyFinding reusing runRefuters/majorityRefuted at default count (majority-refuted = auto-close-as-fixed signal); last-seen commit in file_state sentinel row @daemon/last-seen — slash prefix cannot collide with repo paths, avoids a migration; MarkFixed not dismiss for resolved findings so reintroduced bugs can reopen; spend-read errors fail open (per-cycle budget still bounds); hash-unchanged findings never touched; survivors re-anchored to new hash to avoid per-cycle re-verification.

title: Evaluation harness: seeded-bug benchmarks, precision/recall
description: Ground truth for tuning precision: mutation/bug-injection benchmark over healthy fixture repos measuring detection rate AND false-positive rate on unmutated code; regression corpus of real confirmed bugs; runnable in CI via recorded LLM transcripts (from harness transcript capture) so results are cheap and comparable.
acceptance_criteria: Single command produces precision/recall numbers per lens and per stage; offline mode runs from recorded fixtures with no API calls; results comparable across runs for prompt/pipeline regression detection.
close_reason: Eval harness built and verified (28 tests, -race; suite runs at precision 1.000, tp=5 fp=0 fn=0). THE command: go test ./internal/eval/ -run TestBenchmarkSuite -v. Decisions: recorded replay is two-level (role, run-sequence)->session because funnel shares one client per role across runners; MaxParallel=1 default in eval since parallelism breaks sequence-matched replay; bug Kind is a label not a match gate (file+line matching only) so cross-lens detection still counts; vacuous-perfect convention avoids NaN on clean cases; suppressed case asserted to die in triage not verification. Real-model transcript corpus is future work; mechanism proven with synthetic recordings. RunSuite ready for a future bugbot eval command.

title: Reporting: markdown + SARIF emitters, sinks, report CLI, suppression
description: Report generation and management: markdown and SARIF emitters (reasoning chain, tier, repro artifact link); pluggable sink interface (filesystem, stdout now; GitHub issues/webhook later); bugbot report list/show/dismiss CLI where dismiss writes suppression memory.
acceptance_criteria: Emitted SARIF validates against the SARIF 2.1 schema; dismiss causes the finding to be suppressed in subsequent scans; markdown report contains file:line, tier, reasoning chain, and repro link when present.
close_reason: Reporting built and verified (golden markdown, SARIF shape tests, FSSink + latest copies, dismiss->IsSuppressed flow against real store, ID prefix resolution). Decisions: dismiss uses AddSuppression (suppression row + status flip in one txn, the path UpsertFinding honors forever) not UpdateStatus; package store-decoupled via 1-method findingLister so daemon reuses CollectOpen cycle-free; SARIF validation by-construction with schema validation deferred to CI; legacy markdown/sarif sink names aliased to fs, config default/template updated to fs by orchestrator.

title: Reproduce stage: sandboxed failing-test generation (Tier 1)
description: For verified findings, a reproducer agent writes a minimal failing test or repro script and executes it in the sandbox. Demonstrated failure promotes the finding to Tier 1 with the artifact attached; failed repro demotes to T2 but does not delete.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded-bug fixture yields at least one T1 finding with a runnable repro artifact; failed repro attempts demote rather than drop; artifacts stored and linked from reports.
close_reason: Repro stage built and verified (unit suite race-clean with scripted client + sandbox mock; integration test against real podman + golang:1.26-alpine behind integration tag, offline-capable fixture). Decisions: promotion ruleset is conservative — non-zero exit with build/dep markers treated as failed attempt not demonstration (false-repro guard errs toward demotion); failed repro demotes nothing (T2 stays); Attempt is store-free, PromoteAll owns persistence and surfaces persist-failures as errors; store untouched — promotion via GetFindingByFingerprint + UpsertFinding re-read to avoid clobbering; default parallelism 2 due to workspace copy cost.

title: Regression hunter: findings introduced within a commit range
description: Answer 'what got worse since X' instead of 'what is wrong'. 'bugbot regress --from A --to B' (and a daemon weekly report): restrict scanning to the blast radius of A..B (the --since machinery generalized to a range), then attribute each finding as INTRODUCED (absent at A, present at B — re-verify the anchor against A's content) vs PRE-EXISTING. Daemon emits a per-cycle/per-week 'new since last green' digest. Synergy: the git history tools + churn heat prioritization planned in bugbot-sro provide the plumbing (commit-range diffing, blame); build on it.
acceptance_criteria: regress command scans only the A..B blast radius and labels findings introduced vs pre-existing with the evidence (anchor check at A); daemon digest lists introduced-this-period findings; works when A's tree lacks the file (treated as introduced); fixture test with a bug added in a known commit; tree green + lint clean.

title: Doc-contract drift lens: verify code honors its documented contracts
description: Bugs linters cannot see by construction: doc comments state contracts ('caller must hold mu', 'returns nil when X', 'safe for concurrent use', 'must be closed by caller') that the implementation or callers silently violate. New built-in lens: finder extracts explicit contract claims from doc comments in the chunk, then uses the code-nav tools (find_references — now cheap) to check callers/implementation against each claim. High-precision target: only EXPLICIT normative statements (must/never/always/safe-for), not vibes. The committed M3 corpus and this repo's own internal/lsp (rich doc contracts) are good first targets.
acceptance_criteria: Lens ships as a built-in; finder prompt extracts only explicit normative claims and verifies via code-nav with the claim quoted in the candidate; fixture eval case with a seeded doc-contract violation (e.g. 'must hold mu' violated) is found and survives refutation; no measurable precision regression on the recorded corpus replay; tree green + lint clean.

title: Repo-specific lens generation: meta-agent writes custom lenses
description: Generic lenses find generic bugs; generated lenses find THIS repo's bugs. A meta-agent runs once per repo (and on significant change): reads README/CLAUDE.md/doc comments/build files, identifies frameworks, conventions, and invariants ('shells out to podman — audit arg construction'; 'store dedups by fingerprint — check every insert computes it'), and emits custom lens definitions (name, finder prompt, scope hints) persisted in the store and versioned. Generated lenses run alongside built-ins, clearly labeled in findings. Human can list/disable them ('bugbot lens list/disable'). Guardrails: cap count, require the meta-agent to cite the evidence (file:line) each lens is derived from, and never let a generated lens widen scan scope beyond config.
acceptance_criteria: Meta-agent produces N<=cap lenses each citing derivation evidence; persisted + versioned in store; sweep runs them alongside built-ins with provenance labels in report; lens list/disable works; regeneration is idempotent on an unchanged repo; tree green + lint clean.

title: Per-lens precision calibration: auto-tune verification spend
description: Track each lens's historical precision (from eval corpus replays + human verdicts: dismissals vs confirmed/published findings) in the store, and let the funnel auto-tune refuter count per lens: noisy lenses get more adversarial verification, consistently-precise lenses get the minimum. Keep it transparent: scan summary shows effective refuter counts and why. Bounded: never below 1 or above configured max; cold-start (no history) uses the configured default. Depends on dismissal data existing (false-positive memory bead).
acceptance_criteria: Store accumulates per-lens outcome counts; funnel derives refuter count per lens within configured bounds; summary/report show the derived counts with the precision stats behind them; deterministic given fixed history (unit test); cold-start behaves exactly as today; tree green + lint clean.

title: SARIF export for GitHub code scanning and tool interop
description: Findings -> SARIF 2.1.0 so results land in GitHub's Security/code-scanning tab and interoperate with other analysis tooling. 'bugbot export --format sarif' (and a scan flag) mapping: lens -> rule, tier -> level (T1/T2 -> error/warning, T3 -> note), fingerprint -> partialFingerprints, reasoning -> message, file:line -> physicalLocation. Keep it dependency-light (hand-rolled struct marshaling; the schema subset we emit is small).
acceptance_criteria: Exported SARIF validates against the 2.1.0 schema (testdata golden file + a validator in tests if feasible); uploadable via gh code-scanning upload on a fixture run; stable fingerprints across runs; tree green + lint clean.

title: LSP conn robustness: async server-request replies; prefer stderr-tail crash errors over bare EOF
description: Two low-severity findings from the bugbot-tmb code review (PR #5), deferred. (1) conn.readLoop answers server->client requests synchronously under wmu (conn.go respondToServer): a misbehaving server that stops reading our stdin while still sending requests blocks the write, stops the read loop draining, and wedges the connection until process exit — callers stay bounded by the 60s query timeout but the read goroutine leaks until process death. Fix: route replies through a buffered channel drained by a dedicated writer goroutine, or make reply writes best-effort/non-blocking. (2) markDead is once-guarded and races between readLoop (bare EOF) and the cmd.Wait goroutine (which carries the stderr tail explaining the crash); EOF often wins so the user-facing error drops the diagnostic stderr. Fix: have the EOF path defer briefly to the process-exit error, or prefer a non-EOF deadErr.
acceptance_criteria: Reply write-back cannot stall the read loop (test: server sends requests but never reads stdin; conn keeps draining and Close stays bounded); crash errors include the stderr tail deterministically (test: crashing fake server with stderr output, assert tail present in the query error); -race clean; tree green + lint clean.

title: Daemon-lifetime LSP server manager: keep language-server indexes warm across cycles
description: bugbot-tmb (PR #5) spawns and shuts down language servers per scan/cycle via Funnel.Close(). gopls indexing takes seconds-to-minutes on medium repos, so per-cycle spawn re-pays warm-up every cycle. Lift the lsp.Manager to daemon lifetime: construct once at daemon boot, share across cycles, pre-warm at boot, shut down on daemon exit. Was in the original v2 scoping ('daemon mode is the natural fit: start servers at boot, amortize indexing warm-up across cycles').
acceptance_criteria: Daemon constructs one lsp.Manager at boot and reuses it across cycles (verify gopls PID stable across two cycles in an integration test or log assertion); clean shutdown on daemon exit incl. signal paths; one-shot scan behavior unchanged (per-scan lifecycle); crash-restart semantics still bounded; tree green + lint clean.

title: Give repro agents the code-nav tools
description: internal/repro builds its own readOnlyTools(dir) (repro.go:258) and was an intentional scope cut in bugbot-tmb (PR #5): repro agents get read/list/grep but not find_definition/find_references/find_implementations. Add a CodeNav instance (with Close hook) to the repro tool set so reproduction agents can navigate precisely too.
acceptance_criteria: Repro agent tool set includes the three code-nav tools backed by the shared lsp.Manager pattern; CodeNav closed on all repro exit paths; existing repro tests pass; tree green + lint clean.

value: Git workflow on this repo (github.com/dpoage/bugbot, default branch main): direct 'git push origin main' is BLOCKED by permission classifier — push a feature branch instead ('git push origin main:branch-name'), 'gh pr create', merge with 'gh pr merge N --rebase --delete-branch'. Rebase-merge rewrites SHAs: afterwards 'git fetch && git diff main origin/main --quiet && git reset --hard origin/main' to re-sync. CI = build/vet/test + golangci-lint v2 via action@v8; run lint locally exactly as CI: 'go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@latest run' (config has build-tags integration,live,record).

Live/eval invocations: live API probe = 'source ~/.config/bugbot/env && LLM_LIVE_BASE_URL=https://api.minimax.io/v1 LLM_LIVE_MODEL=MiniMax-M3 LLM_LIVE_API_KEY=$MINIMAX_API_KEY go test -tags live -run TestLive -v ./internal/llm/'. Corpus recorder (REAL spend, ~15min/3 cases): same env, 'go test -tags record -run TestRecordCorpus -v ./internal/eval/' writes internal/eval/testdata/recorded/. Offline replay: 'go test -run TestRecordedCorpusReplay ./internal/eval/' or 'bugbot eval --recorded'. Scripted gate: 'bugbot eval'. Calibration config recipe (old one was in /tmp, ephemeral): provider type openai-compatible + base_url + api_key_env MINIMAX_API_KEY, roles all MiniMax-M3, scope scan.include tightly and exclude **/*_test.go — budget overshoot (bugbot-zrr) means real runs can exceed per_cycle_tokens ~2.7x until fixed.

MiniMax API key (user-provided coding-plan key, OK for Bugbot use) lives in ~/.config/bugbot/env (chmod 600, outside git) as 'export MINIMAX_API_KEY=...'. Source it before live runs. Endpoint: https://api.minimax.io/v1 (OpenAI-compatible; /v1/models works). Models: MiniMax-M3 (validated: clean tool-calling, <think> blocks in content), plus M2.x family. Never put the key in bugbot.yaml or git — config references it via api_key_env.

Session bootstrap: 'bd ready' is the queue. Calibration prerequisites ALL DONE (2026-06-10, PRs 4-9 merged): prompt caching, LSP code-nav, tree-sitter fallback, finder reliability, budget pool, live-probe validation (bugbot-7f0), cross-lens dedup (bugbot-j5e, PR #9 squash 255ad25 — recorded resource-leak now pins 1tp/1fp). Queue is now P2 features: bugbot-n8f patch-prover, bugbot-0q8 PR review mode, bugbot-dx1 gh issue publishing, bugbot-dbg OAuth auth, etc. — pick by value, no hard ordering. Orchestration pattern that works: implementer agent in isolated worktree -> code-reviewer agent on diff (finds real issues most rounds) -> orchestrator applies small review fixes itself + re-runs gates in worktree -> push branch -> PR -> merge (ask user; auto-merge denied by classifier unless user pre-authorizes; user accepted squash-merge for PR 9) -> git worktree remove before deleting local branch -> close bead with decision record. Note: 'bd dolt push' is a no-op (no dolt remote); beads state rides in .beads/issues.jsonl via git, and a beads-only commit pushed directly to main was allowed.

Host environment: podman 5.8.2 (NO docker daemon) — sandbox uses CLI backend, integration tests 'go test -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/ ./internal/repro/' (~25s, images alpine + golang:1.26-alpine already pulled). Go toolchain go1.26.4 custom build; go.mod says 1.26.4. MiniMax-M3 operational notes: spend is input-dominated ~130:1 (no prompt caching on openai-compatible — biggest cost lever, see bugbot-zrr); finder agents on >10-file chunks run 12-30min and exhaust iterations/think-tokens ending with unparseable output (bugbot-210 is the fix; until then keep scan scopes tiny and expect 'No findings' to possibly mean 'finders failed' — check the skip/error lines).
